eRefunds

eRefunds puts money in your account… FAST!

No more trips to your financial institution or waiting for a paper check to come in the mail. Direct Deposit is the secure and convenient way to get your refund.

  • Apply now

Bursar FAQs

    

CONTACT INFORMATION

Where is the Office of the Bursar located on campus?

The Office of the Bursar is located in the Student Mall under the parking deck on Summit St.  You may access the office by using parking Lot 3.

How do I contact The Office of the Bursar?

If you have any billing questions, please visit our office in the Student Mall.  We can be reached via email (bursar@njit.edu) or call us at 973-596-2877.

What time does The Office of Bursar close?

The Office of the Bursar is open Monday - Friday from 8:30am - 4:30pm.  At the start of each semester, the office will be open some late nights to accommodate students.  Holiday and Summer schedule may affect hours of operation.

 

When can I pick up petty cash?

Petty cash is disbursed between 9:00am - 3:30pm, Monday - Friday.  Hours are subject to change during the Holidays.  Please make sure your form is complete with authorized signatures, as well as original receipts.

 

I have questions about my financial aid.

Financial Aid questions should be directed to the Financial Aid office. Contact information for the Financial Aid Office.

PAYMENTS

I know eStatements are new this year can you explain them to me?

We have created an entire section on the webpage just for eStatement questions. Please click here to be directed to eStatements.

How much is the Tuition?

The current tuition and fee rates for undergraduate and graduate students can be found here.

What types of payment do you accept?

NJIT accepts cash (for coins - up to $5 rolled), check (return check fees apply), and money order. You may also send a check or money order via mail. Credit cards are accepted through our payment portal by logging in to MyNJIT.

How do I pay my bill?

Please click here for instructions.

How do I set up a payment plan?

Please click here for instructions.

I am an International Student can I send money through a Wire Transfer?

Please go to our International payments page.

Do I have to pay my bill all at once?

Payment Plans are available each semester. Please review the current options for Payment Plans.

My employer is paying for my tuition, what do I have to do?

Please review the information on Third Party Payments.

Can I pay my bill over the phone?

Unfortunately, NJIT does not accept payment over the telephone, but please see all the other available Payment Options.

I’m on a payment plan. Why do I have a bursar hold on my account?

All accounts with balances are placed on AR-Accounts Receivable hold after the 100% period.

Can my hold be removed so I can access my grades temporarily?

Please contact bursar@njit.edu.

What is the university fee? Why am I charged a university fee as an online student?

Select Tuition and Fee Schedule for more information.

The purpose of this fee is to continue to help support a portion of the costs associated with an array of varied, but integral services and projects that directly affect our students. All students are charged the University Fee.

LATE PAYMENTS

What happens if I do not pay my bill by the due date?

The student will be subject to a $250 Payment Penalty Fee.

If I make a partial payment, will I still be charged a late fee?

Accounts will be charged the $250 payment penalty if they are not fully covered, partial payments are not considered fully covered. Please review the NJIT Payment Policy for Tuition and Fees.

Can you remove my hold so I can register even though I owe a balance?

NJIT Policy is that all students must be paid in full to have a hold removed.

Can you remove my late registration fee?

No, late registration fees may not be removed. You may contest it by filling out a hardship request form.

REFUNDS

Can my refund be sent through direct deposit? Can I pick up the check in person?

All refund checks are mailed to the student address on file in their MyNJIT account. Checks may not be picked up.

Can I have 100% refund even though I dropped the class after the 100% refund period ended?

Refunds are based upon the academic calendar, please contact the Registrar's office.

I dropped in the 80% refund period. Why am I being charged the fees?

Refunds are based upon the academic calendar. Fees are not refunded after the 100% refund period.

I never made a payment and I never attended the class. Why am I still being billed?

Students are responsible for all courses registered for and not dropped. You may contact the Registrars Office and/or complete a hardship appeal.

I never used the student health insurance. Can I get a refund for it?

No, refunds on health insurance are not issued.  Please visit the Student Health Insurance website for more information.

I started a payment plan, then dropped my classes during the 100% refund period. Can you refund me the payment plan fee?

Payment Plan fees are non-refundable.

Where is my refund?

Please visit the Refund Section of our website.

What is the difference between partial and full withdrawal and how does that affect the amount of tuition refunded?

  • Full withdraw is when you withdraw from ALL of your courses
  • Partial withdraw is when you withdraw from SOME of your courses.
  • Check the Registrar Academic Calendar for refund information.

TAX FORMS

Can you print an official copy of my 1098T?

1098T's can be viewed/printed by going to MyNJIT Account > Student Services > Bursar's Office > Student Account and Payment Portal > Continue

 

PETTY CASH

What are the Petty Cash Guidelines?

  • Petty Cash Hours: Monday – Friday, 9am-3:30 (Reduced Days & Hours during summer)
  • The person receiving petty cash cannot be the same as the approver
  • The person picking up the petty cash cannot be the same as the approver
  • Approver must be a supervisor at least one level up.
  • Online expenses over 60 days must be reimbursed via chrome river
  • All other expenses over 90 days must be reimbursed via chrome river
  • Petty cash is limited to $150 a day, per person, per index.
  • Pick-up person is required to show school ID
  • All receipts and signatures must be originals
  • The Petty Cash form must be completed in its entirety. 
  • Students get reimbursed for taxes; unless the expense is for food.

AUTHORIZED USER

How to setup Authorized user

Please visit the Setup Authorized User Section of our website

Link for Authorized User to login into Touchnet

Independent access link for authorized users can be found in the For Parents section of the Bursar website: Authorized User Login

BURSAR HOME
